title: Judith Ousting Minotaur
description: This work reimagines Judith Beheading Holofernes through a playful and contemporary lens. Drawing from the dramatic intensity and chiaroscuro of Caravaggio, the piece transforms the original narrative into a whimsical yet layered allegory of revelation and disguise. In this reinterpretation, Judith's decisive act is prompted not by political or heroic necessity, but by an unexpected and almost absurd chain of discovery: a domestic moment in which her cat captures a mouse, the mouse uncovers sunflower seeds, and the seeds ultimately expose her lover as a Minotaur in disguise. The intrusion of the uncanny into the intimate space reframes the scene, merging humor with tension. By juxtaposing Baroque drama with surreal storytelling, the work explores themes of perception, hidden identity, and the fragile boundary between the ordinary and the fantastical.

title: Violin Performance
description: This painting of a violin was created as part of a visual performance presented by the artist, Sarah, in collaboration with several colleagues for an orchestral reception and pre-show preceding the formal concert. The instrument depicted belongs to one of her fellow performers, grounding the work in a personal and collaborative context. As a still life, the piece reflects the evolving bond between two individuals whose distinct beginnings converge through their shared dedication to the arts. It speaks to the unifying power of creative practice, illustrating how artistic spaces foster connection, mutual understanding, and parallel journeys.

title: Desk Still Life
description: This painting presents a focused study of the corner of a desk, examining the interplay of light, form, and spatial composition within a familiar setting. The work employs the Verdaccio technique, utilizing a green-toned underpainting to establish a cohesive value structure and sense of depth. Subsequent layers are developed through glazing, allowing for subtle modulation of tone and color while preserving the integrity of the underlying forms.

title: Park Still Life
description: This painting presents a close-up study of a weathered playground structure, drawing attention to surface texture, form, and the passage of time. The work employs the Grisaille technique, using a monochromatic range of black and white values to establish a strong underpainting that defines depth, contrast, and structural clarity. Layers of glazing are applied over this foundation, enriching tonal variation and subtly building color while preserving the integrity of the underlying value structure.

title: Color Theory Study
description: This painting was developed as part of a coursework assignment completed by Sarah at the University of Central Florida. The composition juxtaposes natural, organic forms—such as flowers—with the angular, structural geometry of man-made architecture, creating a visual dialogue between the organic and the constructed. The work emphasizes the application of color theory, integrating analogous, monochromatic, tertiary, and complementary color harmonies to achieve a cohesive and visually engaging composition.

title: Koi Fish and Orchids
description: This mural was commissioned by an award-winning business as part of a two-piece installation designed to enrich the environment and elevate the overall atmosphere. As the second of the two murals, it plays a key role in creating a visually engaging space that invites connection and interaction within the community. The imagery centers on a koi pond, a subject chosen with intention and cultural significance. Koi fish are often associated with prosperity, good fortune, and abundance, symbolizing the client’s aspirations for continued success and positive energy within their space. Through this work, the mural not only enhances the aesthetic experience but also reflects a deeper narrative of growth, luck, and community engagement.

title: Purple Orchids
description: This painting was commissioned by an award-winning business as the first of two murals created to enhance the space and elevate its atmosphere. As the first mural to be painted, it serves as a foundational visual element, thoughtfully designed to create an inviting and engaging environment that fosters a sense of connection within the community. The imagery features the business’s favorite flower, the orchid, which appears throughout the space as a unifying motif. The intention was to emphasize the elegance of the orchid while seamlessly tying it into the surrounding foliage found across the business. Orchids are often associated with love, luxury, and beauty—qualities that reflect the essence of the nail salon. Through this floral theme, the mural captures and expresses the refined, welcoming experience the business aims to provide.

title: Backyard Duck
description: This painting was created as a heartfelt gift for Sarah’s father, inspired by his deep appreciation for the vibrant life within his backyard, a space filled with lush plants, fruit-bearing trees, and the presence of a beloved 'little' duck. The composition reflects not only the physical beauty of the garden, but also the sense of joy and care that defined it. Following her parents’ move from this cherished home, the painting now serves as a lasting tribute to a place rich with personal meaning. As time passes and circumstances evolve, such pieces become important anchors to memory. Whether through a photograph, written word, or painting, these tangible reminders offer comfort and connection to moments and spaces that continue to live on beyond change.

title: Alto Saxophone
description: Similar to the violin painting, this work depicts another of Sarah’s friend’s instruments, continuing an exploration of personal objects as artistic subjects. While it shares a thematic connection to the earlier piece, it was created under a different context and intention. This painting functioned primarily as a study, focusing on the challenges of rendering metallic surfaces and refining an understanding of color relationships. Through this process, the work emphasizes observation, technique, and material translation, serving as both a technical exercise and an extension of the artist’s ongoing exploration of form and light.

title: Self Portrait
description: This work is a self-portrait by the artist, Sarah, exploring the expressive potential of light and form. The use of harsh, directional lighting creates a heightened sense of drama while emphasizing the structural planes of the face. Rooted in direct observation, the piece prioritizes what is visually perceived over preconceived notions, resulting in a study that is both disciplined and perceptually driven.

title: Bones Still Life
description: This work is a study of both human and animal anatomy, focusing on structure, proportion, and comparative form. The selective use of purple introduces a subtle contrast, enhancing visual interest while drawing attention to key areas within the composition.

title: Portrait Study #2
description: This work is an academic portrait study of a live model, emphasizing careful observation and technical precision. Through the application of color theory and pastel drawing techniques, the artist builds nuanced, lifelike skin tones, creating a sense of depth and presence on the paper.

title: Moving Figure
description: This work is a dynamic figure study capturing a model in motion, with poses changing at twenty-minute intervals. The resulting composition reads as a sequential, animation-like drawing, conveying movement and the passage of time through repeated forms. The use of color serves to articulate and define the contours of the body, enhancing the sense of structure and continuity across each pose.

title: Model and Still Life #8
description: This drawing originates from a live figure session incorporating still-life elements, including human and animal skeletons. The composition emphasizes accuracy in the rendering of each figure, while the centrally positioned skeleton is developed with greater definition to guide the viewer’s attention. This contrast in emphasis establishes a clear focal point and reinforces the structural relationships within the scene.

title: Model and Still Life #4
description: This drawing originates from a live figure session that incorporated both human and still-life elements, including surrounding plant forms. The composition explores spatial depth through tonal contrast, with the foreground plant rendered in darker values and the figure in lighter tones to suggest its placement further in the background. Particular emphasis is placed on the treatment of light across the model’s back, where highlights are used both to define the musculature and to guide the viewer’s eye. The selective use of white charcoal in this area creates a focal point, drawing attention to the form while reinforcing the overall sense of depth and composition.

title: Portrait Study #5
description: This work is an academic portrait study of a live model, emphasizing careful observation and technical precision. Through the application of color theory and pastel drawing techniques, the artist builds nuanced, lifelike skin tones, creating a sense of depth and presence on the paper.

title: Morning Coffee
description: This photograph was captured during a morning coffee outing in Huế, Vietnam, documenting a quiet, everyday moment. Bright sunlight filters across plants and motorbikes, creating a dynamic interplay of light and shadow that animates the scene. The warm, earthy tones of the environment evoke a sense of calm, reflecting the atmosphere of a peaceful outdoor coffee experience.

title: City View Series-22
description: This photograph is part of a larger series documenting urban landscapes across Vietnam. The project began during Sarah’s travels through her family’s home country, where she spent a month exploring regions in the north, central, and southern parts of the country. The series reflects her observations and experiences, capturing the character and visual richness of the cities she encountered, while also serving as a personal exploration of place, culture, and connection.

title: Tranquil Meal
description: This photograph captures a quiet, contemplative moment of a peaceful, unhurried lunch. The architecture of the restaurant subtly frames the composition, guiding the viewer’s eye through the scene. Earthy green tones contrast with the red brick structure in the background, creating a balanced interplay of color and enhancing the sense of calm within the environment.

title: City View Series-13
description: This photograph is part 13 of the City View Series. Captured from within a hotel interior, the composition centers on a large window that frames the exterior urban landscape. The contrast between the dark interior and the illuminated outdoor scene introduces a sense of drama, emphasizing depth and spatial tension. Sarah’s approach to street photography is evident in her attention to line and structure, using architectural elements to guide the viewer’s eye and create a sense of visual rhythm throughout the composition.

title: Rusted Farmhouse
description: This photograph was taken outside the home of Sarah’s grandmother in Huế, Vietnam, serving as a deeply personal reflection within her broader body of work. As she traveled throughout the country, the experience prompted a consideration of her family’s origins—from modest beginnings to the journey of rebuilding a life in the United States. The image evokes a sense of stillness and simplicity, recalling quieter rhythms of the past while honoring the resilience and history embedded in place. It functions as both a moment of reflection and a visual acknowledgment of heritage, memory, and the enduring connection to one’s roots.

title: Woman Selling at Market
description: This photograph was taken while walking through the markets of Huế, Vietnam, just steps from Sarah’s grandfather’s home. Surrounded by displays of freshly caught fish, vegetables, and preserved goods, the scene captures the rhythms of daily life and the labor that sustains it. In engaging with this environment, Sarah reflects on a sense of empathy and connection to the individuals whose livelihoods unfold within these spaces. The experience prompts a deeper consideration of identity and circumstance—an awareness of her family’s choices to build a life in the United States, alongside a contemplation of the alternate paths her life might have taken. The image becomes both a document of place and a meditation on gratitude, possibility, and belonging.

title: ML Pro Nails & Spa Brand Identity
description: This project presents a comprehensive brand identity system developed for a nail salon business, encompassing posters, social media content, and a range of promotional materials. The design approach integrates cohesive visual language across typography, color palette, layout, and graphic elements to ensure consistency and brand recognition. The visual direction prioritizes an elegant and refined aesthetic, reflecting the client’s vision of a tasteful, elevated experience. Careful attention is given to brand voice, tone, and overall presentation, resulting in a cohesive and recognizable identity that translates seamlessly across both print and digital platforms.

title: Dynamics Lion Dance Brand Identity
description: Full brand identity system for a botanical skincare startup...

title: Hero's Aid
description: Full brand identity system for a botanical skincare startup...
